threat_model.name: ALdeci Platform Self-Threat Model
threat_model.scope: ALdeci CTEM+ Decision Intelligence Platform — full attack surface

deployment.database: SQLite WAL (34 .db files across data/, .fixops_data/, suite-api/data/)
deployment.auth: API key header (X-API-Key) + JWT HS256 tokens
deployment.tls: Terminated at reverse proxy (no built-in TLS)

trust_boundaries.name: Internet → API Gateway
trust_boundaries.description: External requests hit FastAPI through reverse proxy

trust_boundaries.name: API Gateway → Core Engines
trust_boundaries.description: Validated requests dispatched to business logic

trust_boundaries.name: Core Engines → External Services
trust_boundaries.description: MPTE, DAST, Connectors reach external targets

trust_boundaries.name: MCP Gateway → AI Agents
trust_boundaries.description: AI agents consume security tools via MCP protocol

trust_boundaries.name: Sandbox → Docker Containers
trust_boundaries.description: PoC verification runs in isolated Docker containers

data_flows.data: API requests, scan targets, code snippets

threats.title: API Key Theft via Default Token in Codebase
threats.description: The default API token is hardcoded in multiple scripts and test files. An attacker who obtains the codebase (open-source or leaked) can authenticate as any user.
mitigations: Rotate default token before any external deployment
Implement per-user API key management with database-backed storage
Add token expiration and rotation policies
Remove hardcoded tokens from all scripts — use environment variables exclusively

threats.title: SQLite Database Tampering via Direct File Access
threats.description: 34 SQLite database files are stored on local filesystem with standard file permissions. A compromised process or container escape gives full read/write access to all finding data, evidence, and configuration.
mitigations: Encrypt SQLite databases at rest using SQLCipher
Restrict file permissions to application user only
Implement database integrity checks on startup
Add audit logging for all database write operations

threats.title: Evidence Signing Key Generated Ephemeral Without HSM
threats.description: RSA signing keys for evidence bundles are generated at runtime and stored on the filesystem. No HSM or KMS integration. An attacker who gains filesystem access can forge evidence bundles.
mitigations: Integrate with AWS KMS / Azure Key Vault / HashiCorp Vault for key storage
Implement key rotation with versioned signing
Add timestamping authority (TSA) integration for non-repudiation
Hardware Security Module (HSM) for production deployments

threats.title: Customer Code Leakage to External LLM Providers
threats.description: AutoFix engine sends customer code snippets to external LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic) for fix generation. Code snippets may contain proprietary algorithms, secrets, or regulated data (PHI, PCI).
mitigations: Implement data classification tags — never send PCI/PHI-classified code externally
Add air-gapped mode using local LLM (Ollama/vLLM) for sensitive environments
Redact secrets and credentials from code snippets before LLM submission
Add customer consent workflow for external LLM code analysis
Log all external LLM API calls with data classification audit trail

threats.title: Brain Pipeline O(n²) Graph Step Causes Resource Exhaustion
threats.description: The build_graph step in the Brain Pipeline uses O(n²) algorithm for knowledge graph construction. With large finding sets (>10,000), this can exhaust CPU and memory, blocking all subsequent requests since the application is single-threaded.
mitigations: Implement pagination/batching for graph construction
Add finding count limits per pipeline run (configurable)
Move brain pipeline to background worker (async execution)
Add circuit breaker: abort graph step if >30s elapsed

threats.title: MPTE Scanner Used as SSRF Proxy to Internal Networks
threats.description: The MPTE comprehensive scan accepts user-supplied target URLs. An authenticated attacker can scan internal network hosts (169.254.169.254 metadata service, internal microservices) using ALdeci as a proxy.
mitigations: Implement strict SSRF allowlist for MPTE/DAST target URLs
Block RFC 1918 private ranges, link-local (169.254.x.x), localhost
Block cloud metadata endpoints (169.254.169.254, metadata.google.internal)
Add DNS rebinding protection — resolve and validate IP before connecting
Rate limit MPTE scans per user/org (max 10/hour)

threats.title: JWT Secret Auto-Generated Without Rotation
threats.description: JWT signing secret is auto-generated on first startup and persisted to .fixops_data/.jwt_secret file. No rotation mechanism exists. If the secret is compromised, all JWTs are forgeable indefinitely.
mitigations: Implement JWT key rotation (e.g., every 24 hours with grace period)
Use asymmetric signing (RS256) so public key can be distributed safely
Store JWT secret in external KMS, not filesystem
Add JTI (JWT ID) claim for token revocation capability

threats.title: Malicious Code Injection via SAST Scan Input
threats.description: SAST scanner accepts arbitrary code snippets via POST /api/v1/sast/scan/code. While the scanner parses code using AST, a crafted input could trigger Python's ast.parse or exec vulnerabilities, or cause regex catastrophic backtracking in vulnerability pattern matching.
mitigations: Limit input code size (max 1MB)
Add timeout to AST parsing (max 5s per scan)
Run SAST engine in sandboxed subprocess with resource limits
Test regex patterns for ReDoS vulnerability
Never execute or eval scanned code — parse only

threats.title: MCP Tool Discovery Exposes Internal API Surface to AI Agents
threats.description: The MCP endpoint GET /api/v1/mcp/tools returns all 100 available security tools to any authenticated AI agent. This reveals the complete internal API surface, including offensive security tools (MPTE, DAST, Sandbox) that an adversarial AI agent could weaponize.
mitigations: Implement tool-level RBAC — restrict offensive tools to admin-level tokens
Add MCP agent registration with capability-based permissions
Log all MCP tool invocations with agent identity
Rate limit MCP tool calls per agent session

threats.title: Rate Limiting Bypass via Environment Variable
threats.description: Rate limiting can be completely disabled by setting FIXOPS_DISABLE_RATE_LIMIT=1. If this environment variable is accidentally set in production, the API has zero rate limiting protection against DDoS or brute-force attacks.
mitigations: Remove FIXOPS_DISABLE_RATE_LIMIT environment variable override
Implement defense-in-depth rate limiting (API gateway + application + database)
Add startup warning if rate limiting is disabled
Require explicit configuration override in config file, not env var

threats.title: Sandbox Container Escape via Docker Socket Access
threats.description: The sandbox verifier creates Docker containers for PoC verification. If the host Docker socket is mounted or the container has elevated privileges, an attacker could escape the sandbox and access the host system.
mitigations: Use rootless Docker or Podman for sandbox containers
Never mount Docker socket into sandbox containers
Apply seccomp and AppArmor profiles to sandbox containers
Use gVisor or Kata Containers for additional isolation
Set CPU/memory/network limits on all sandbox containers

threats.title: Threat Feed Cache Contains Stale Vulnerability Data
threats.description: NVD/EPSS/KEV feeds are cached locally in SQLite databases. If feed updates fail silently, the platform may report outdated risk scores, missing critical CVEs, or incorrect EPSS probabilities — leading to wrong triage decisions.
mitigations: Add feed staleness alerts (>24h without update = warning, >72h = error)
Display last-updated timestamp prominently in all risk assessments
Implement feed health monitoring with automatic retry and fallback
Tag all findings with feed_freshness metadata

recommendations.title: Rotate Default API Token Before Demo
recommendations.description: Remove hardcoded API tokens from all scripts. Use FIXOPS_API_TOKEN environment variable exclusively. Generate unique token per deployment.

recommendations.title: Implement Code Redaction for External LLM Calls
recommendations.description: Add PII/PHI/PCI detection before sending code to external LLMs. Implement air-gapped LLM option for sensitive environments.

recommendations.title: Harden SSRF Protection on MPTE/DAST
recommendations.description: Implement comprehensive SSRF allowlist. Block private IP ranges, metadata endpoints, and link-local addresses. Add DNS rebinding protection.

recommendations.title: Migrate to External Key Management for Evidence Signing
recommendations.description: Integrate with AWS KMS or HashiCorp Vault for RSA key storage and JWT secret management. Critical for enterprise credibility.

recommendations.title: Implement Brain Pipeline Resource Limits
recommendations.description: Add configurable limits: max 5,000 findings per pipeline run, 30s timeout per step, async execution for large batches.
